QF.id: Terminology in Equity and Debt Markets_35

question.text: Companies Act 2013 allows DVR shares up to what percentage of post-issue paid up capital?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** DVR shares shall not exceed 25% of the total post-issue paid-up capital of the company. Additionally, the company must have paid at least 10% dividend over the preceding 3 years. **The Trap:** The 25% limit prevents excessive dilution of voting power. The 10% dividend requirement ensures the company has a track record. **Cross-Exam Context:** DVR regulations are tested in NISM Series XV Chapter 3. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Tata Motors DVR is a well-known example in India. It typically trades at a 35-45% discount to the regular shares.
